crypto.c(3) Library Functions Manual crypto.c(3)

NAME

crypto.c - 🔐 Client cryptography: handshake integration, X25519 key exchange, and per-session encryption

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void client_crypto_set_mode (uint8_t mode)
Set crypto mode for handshake (encryption + authentication). int client_crypto_init (void)
Initialize client crypto handshake. int client_crypto_handshake (socket_t socket)
Perform crypto handshake with server. bool crypto_client_is_ready (void)
Check if crypto handshake is ready. const crypto_context_t * crypto_client_get_context (void)
Get crypto context for encryption/decryption. int crypto_client_encrypt_packet (const uint8_t *plaintext, size_t plaintext_len, uint8_t *ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_size, size_t *ciphertext_len)
Encrypt a packet for transmission. int crypto_client_decrypt_packet (const uint8_t *ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_len, uint8_t *plaintext, size_t plaintext_size, size_t *plaintext_len)
Decrypt a received packet. void crypto_client_cleanup (void)
Cleanup crypto client resources. bool crypto_client_should_rekey (void)
Check if session rekeying should be triggered. int crypto_client_initiate_rekey (void)
Initiate session rekeying (client-initiated). int crypto_client_process_rekey_request (const uint8_t *packet, size_t packet_len)
Process received REKEY_REQUEST packet from server. int crypto_client_send_rekey_response (void)
Send REKEY_RESPONSE packet to server. int crypto_client_process_rekey_response (const uint8_t *packet, size_t packet_len)
Process received REKEY_RESPONSE packet from server. int crypto_client_send_rekey_complete (void)
Send REKEY_COMPLETE packet to server and commit to new key.

Detailed Description

🔐 Client cryptography: handshake integration, X25519 key exchange, and per-session encryption

CORE RESPONSIBILITIES:

1.
Initialize client crypto context with authentication credentials
2.
Perform cryptographic handshake with server during connection
3.
Manage global crypto context for client connection
4.
Provide encryption/decryption functions for secure packet transmission
5.
Support multiple authentication modes (password, SSH key, passwordless)
6.
Handle session rekeying for long-lived connections

CRYPTOGRAPHIC HANDSHAKE ARCHITECTURE:

The handshake follows a multi-phase protocol:

PHASE 0: PROTOCOL NEGOTIATION:

  • Step 0a: Send client protocol version
  • Step 0b: Receive server protocol version
  • Step 0c: Send client crypto capabilities
  • Step 0d: Receive server crypto parameters

PHASE 1: KEY EXCHANGE:

  • Step 1: Receive server's ephemeral public key and send our public key
  • Client generates ephemeral key pair for this session
  • Both sides derive shared secret using X25519 key exchange

PHASE 2: AUTHENTICATION:

  • Step 2: Receive auth challenge and send response
  • Client signs challenge with identity key (if client has identity key)
  • Server verifies client identity (if whitelist enabled)
  • Step 3: Receive handshake complete message

CRYPTO INITIALIZATION:

The client supports three initialization modes:

1.
SSH KEY MODE (--key specified):
•
Parses Ed25519 private key from file or gpg:keyid format
•
Extracts public key for authentication
•
Supports password-protected keys (SSH agent or prompt)
•
Optional password for dual authentication (key + password)
2.
PASSWORD MODE (--password specified):
•
Uses Argon2id key derivation from shared password
•
Both client and server derive same key from password
•
No identity keys required
3.
PASSWORDLESS MODE (no credentials):
•
Generates random ephemeral keys
•
No long-term identity (no authentication)
•
Suitable for trusted networks or testing

GLOBAL CRYPTO CONTEXT:

The client uses a single global crypto context (g_crypto_ctx):

  • Shared across all connection attempts (reused on reconnection)
  • Initialized once per program execution
  • Cleaned up on program shutdown
  • Stores server connection info for known_hosts verification

SERVER IDENTITY VERIFICATION:

Client verifies server identity using known_hosts:

  • Checks server's identity key against ~/.ascii-chat/known_hosts
  • First connection: Prompts user to accept server key
  • Subsequent connections: Verifies key matches stored value
  • Key mismatch: Warns user about potential MITM attack
  • Optional --server-key for explicit server key verification

CLIENT AUTHENTICATION REQUIREMENTS:

When server requires client authentication (whitelist enabled):

  • Client must provide identity key with --key option
  • Client's public key must be in server's --client-keys list
  • Authentication failure results in connection rejection
  • Interactive prompt warns user if no identity key provided

SESSION REKEYING:

Long-lived connections support periodic rekeying:

  • crypto_client_should_rekey(): Checks if rekey is needed
  • crypto_client_initiate_rekey(): Client-initiated rekeying
  • crypto_client_process_rekey_request(): Handles server-initiated rekey
  • Rekeying refreshes encryption keys without reconnecting

ENCRYPTION/DECRYPTION OPERATIONS:

After handshake completion:

  • crypto_client_encrypt_packet(): Encrypts packets before transmission
  • crypto_client_decrypt_packet(): Decrypts received packets
  • Both functions use global crypto context
  • Automatic passthrough when encryption disabled (--no-encrypt)

ALGORITHM SUPPORT:

The client currently supports:

  • Key Exchange: X25519 (Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman)
  • Cipher: XSalsa20-Poly1305 (Authenticated Encryption)
  • Authentication: Ed25519 (when client has identity key)
  • Key Derivation: Argon2id (for password-based authentication)
  • HMAC: HMAC-SHA256 (for additional integrity protection)

ERROR HANDLING:

Handshake errors are handled appropriately:

  • Server disconnection during handshake: Log and return error
  • Protocol mismatch: Log detailed error and abort connection
  • Authentication failure: Log and return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED
  • Network errors: Detect and handle gracefully (reconnection)
  • Invalid packets: Validate size and format before processing

THREAD SAFETY:

Crypto operations are thread-safe:

  • Global crypto context protected by initialization flag
  • Single crypto context per client process (no concurrent handshakes)
  • Encryption/decryption operations are safe for concurrent use
  • Rekeying operations coordinate with connection thread

INTEGRATION WITH OTHER MODULES:

  • main.c: Calls client_crypto_init() during client startup
  • server.c: Calls client_crypto_handshake() during connection
  • protocol.c: Uses encryption functions for secure packet transmission
  • crypto/handshake.h: Core handshake protocol implementation
  • crypto/keys/keys.h: Key parsing and management functions
  • crypto/known_hosts.h: Server identity verification

WHY THIS MODULAR DESIGN:

Separating cryptographic operations from connection management provides:

  • Clear cryptographic interface for application code
  • Easier handshake protocol evolution
  • Better error isolation and debugging
  • Improved security auditing capabilities
  • Independent testing of crypto functionality

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

October 2025

Version

2.0

See also

server.c For connection establishment and Crypto Module handshake timing

crypto/handshake.h For handshake protocol implementation

crypto/keys/keys.h For key parsing and management

crypto/known_hosts.h For server identity verification

Definition in file src/client/crypto.c.

Function Documentation

int client_crypto_handshake (socket_t socket)

Perform crypto handshake with server. Perform crypto handshake with server

Parameters

socket Connected socket to server

Returns

0 on success, -1 on failure

Definition at line 433 of file src/client/crypto.c.

433                                              {
434   // If client has --no-encrypt, skip handshake entirely
435   if (GET_OPTION(no_encrypt)) {
436     log_debug("Client has --no-encrypt, skipping crypto handshake");
437     return 0;
438   }
439 
440   // If we reach here, crypto must be initialized for encryption
441   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_crypto_lc)) {
442     log_error("Crypto not initialized but server requires encryption");
443     log_error("Server requires encrypted connection but client has no encryption configured");
444     log_error("Use --key to specify a client key or --password for password authentication");
445     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED; // No retry - configuration error
446   }
447 
448   log_debug("Starting crypto handshake with server...");
449 
450   START_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
451 
452   // Step 0a: Send protocol version to server
453   protocol_version_packet_t client_version = {0};
454   client_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);  // Protocol version 1
455   client_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0); // Revision 0
456   client_version.supports_encryption = g_crypto_mode;    // Send crypto mode bitmask
457   client_version.compression_algorithms = 0;             // No compression for now
458   client_version.compression_threshold = 0;
459   client_version.feature_flags = 0;
460 
461   int result = send_protocol_version_packet(socket, &client_version);
462   if (result != 0) {
463     log_error("Failed to send protocol version to server");
464     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
465     return -1;
466   }
467   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Protocol version sent successfully");
468 
469   // Step 0b: Receive server's protocol version
470   packet_type_t packet_type;
471   void *payload = NULL;
472   size_t payload_len = 0;
473 
474   result = receive_packet(socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
475   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
476     log_error("Failed to receive server protocol version (got type %u)", packet_type);
477     log_error("Packet type 0x%x (decimal %u) - Expected 0x%x (decimal %d)", packet_type, packet_type,
478               PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION, PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION);
479     log_error("This suggests a protocol mismatch or packet corruption");
480     log_error("Raw packet type bytes: %02x %02x %02x %02x", (packet_type >> 0) & 0xFF, (packet_type >> 8) & 0xFF,
481               (packet_type >> 16) & 0xFF, (packet_type >> 24) & 0xFF);
482     if (payload) {
483       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
484     }
485     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
486     return -1;
487   }
488 
489   if (payload_len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
490     log_error("Invalid protocol version packet size: %zu, expected %zu", payload_len,
491               sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
492     buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
493     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
494     return -1;
495   }
496 
497   protocol_version_packet_t server_version;
498   memcpy(&server_version, payload, sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
499   buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
500 
501   // Convert from network byte order
502   uint16_t server_proto_version = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_version);
503   uint16_t server_proto_revision = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_revision);
504   uint8_t server_mode = server_version.supports_encryption;
505 
506   log_info("Server protocol version: %u.%u (crypto mode: 0x%02x)", server_proto_version, server_proto_revision,
507            server_mode);
508 
509   // Verify server echoed our mode
510   if (server_mode != g_crypto_mode) {
511     log_error("Server mode mismatch: got 0x%02x, expected 0x%02x", server_mode, g_crypto_mode);
512     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
513     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
514   }
515 
516   // Step 0c: Send crypto capabilities to server
517   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Sending crypto capabilities");
518   crypto_capabilities_packet_t client_caps = {0};
519   client_caps.supported_kex_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(KEX_ALGO_X25519);
520   client_caps.supported_auth_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 | AUTH_ALGO_NONE);
521   client_caps.supported_cipher_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305 | CIPHER_ALGO_NONE);
522   client_caps.requires_verification = 0; // Client doesn't require server verification (uses known_hosts)
523   client_caps.preferred_kex = KEX_ALGO_X25519;
524   client_caps.preferred_auth = ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_AUTH(g_crypto_mode) ? AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 : AUTH_ALGO_NONE;
525   client_caps.preferred_cipher =
526       ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(g_crypto_mode) ? CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305 : CIPHER_ALGO_NONE;
527 
528   result = send_crypto_capabilities_packet(socket, &client_caps);
529   if (result != 0) {
530     log_error("Failed to send crypto capabilities to server");
531     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
532     return -1;
533   }
534   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Crypto capabilities sent successfully");
535 
536   // Step 0d: Receive server's crypto parameters
537   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Receiving server crypto parameters");
538   payload = NULL;
539   payload_len = 0;
540 
541   result = receive_packet(socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
542   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS) {
543     log_error("Failed to receive server crypto parameters (got type %u)", packet_type);
544     if (payload) {
545       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
546     }
547     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
548     return -1;
549   }
550 
551   if (payload_len != sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t)) {
552     log_error("Invalid crypto parameters packet size: %zu, expected %zu", payload_len,
553               sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
554     buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
555     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
556     return -1;
557   }
558 
559   crypto_parameters_packet_t server_params;
560   memcpy(&server_params, payload, sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
561   buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
562 
563   // Convert from network byte order
564   uint16_t kex_pubkey_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_params.kex_public_key_size);
565   uint16_t auth_pubkey_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_params.auth_public_key_size);
566   uint16_t signature_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_params.signature_size);
567   uint16_t shared_secret_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_params.shared_secret_size);
568 
569   log_debug("Server crypto parameters: KEX=%u, Auth=%u, Cipher=%u (key_size=%u, auth_size=%u, sig_size=%u, "
570             "secret_size=%u, verification=%u)",
571             server_params.selected_kex, server_params.selected_auth, server_params.selected_cipher, kex_pubkey_size,
572             auth_pubkey_size, signature_size, shared_secret_size, server_params.verification_enabled);
573   log_debug("Raw server_params.kex_public_key_size = %u (network byte order)", server_params.kex_public_key_size);
574 
575   // Set the crypto parameters in the handshake context
576   result = crypto_handshake_set_parameters(&g_crypto_ctx, &server_params);
577   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
578     FATAL(result, "Failed to set crypto parameters");
579   }
580 
581   // Store verification flag - server will verify client identity (whitelist check)
582   // This is independent of whether server provides its own identity
583   if (server_params.verification_enabled) {
584     g_crypto_ctx.server_uses_client_auth = true;
585     g_crypto_ctx.require_client_auth = true;
586     log_info("Server will verify client identity (whitelist enabled)");
587   }
588 
589   // Validate that server chose algorithms we support
590   if (server_params.selected_kex != KEX_ALGO_X25519) {
591     log_error("Server selected unsupported KEX algorithm: %u", server_params.selected_kex);
592     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
593     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
594   }
595 
596   // Validate cipher selection based on negotiated mode
597   bool expect_cipher = ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(g_crypto_mode);
598   if (expect_cipher && server_params.selected_cipher != CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305) {
599     log_error("Server selected unsupported cipher algorithm: %u", server_params.selected_cipher);
600     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
601     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
602   }
603   if (!expect_cipher && server_params.selected_cipher != CIPHER_ALGO_NONE) {
604     log_error("Server chose cipher %u but client requested no encryption", server_params.selected_cipher);
605     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
606     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
607   }
608 
609   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Protocol negotiation completed successfully");
610 
611   // Step 0.5: Send CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO with expected server key (multi-key support)
612   // This allows the server to select the correct identity key from g_server_identity_keys[]
613   if (g_crypto_ctx.verify_server_key && strlen(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key) > 0) {
614     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Sending expected server key to support multi-key selection");
615 
616     // Parse expected server key to get the Ed25519 public key
617     public_key_t expected_keys[MAX_CLIENTS];
618     size_t num_expected_keys = 0;
619 
620     if (parse_public_keys(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key, expected_keys, &num_expected_keys, MAX_CLIENTS) == 0 &&
621         num_expected_keys > 0) {
622       // Send the first expected key (client should only have one expected server key)
623       // Format: 32-byte Ed25519 public key
624       log_debug("Sending CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO with expected server key for multi-key selection");
625       result = send_packet(socket, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO, expected_keys[0].key, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
626 
627       if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
628         FATAL(result, "Failed to send CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO packet");
629       }
630 
631       // Log the key fingerprint for debugging
632       char hex_key[65];
633       pubkey_to_hex(expected_keys[0].key, hex_key);
634       log_debug("Sent expected server key: %s", hex_key);
635     } else {
636       log_warn("Failed to parse expected server key '%s' - server will use default key",
637                g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key);
638     }
639   }
640 
641   // Step 1: Receive server's public key and send our public key
642   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Starting key exchange");
643   result = crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange_socket(&g_crypto_ctx, socket);
644   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
645 #ifdef _WIN32
646     // On Windows: Cleanup capture resources before exiting to prevent Media Foundation threads from hanging exit()
647     // Media Foundation creates background COM threads that can block exit() if not properly shut down
648     capture_cleanup();
649 #endif
650     FATAL(result, "Crypto key exchange failed");
651   }
652   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Key exchange completed successfully");
653 
654   // SECURITY: Warn when server requires client verification but client has no identity key
655   bool client_has_identity = (g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.type == KEY_TYPE_ED25519);
656 
657   if (g_crypto_ctx.require_client_auth && !client_has_identity) {
658     // Server requires client verification but client has no identity key
659     log_warn("Server requires client verification but client has no identity key");
660 
661     // Check if we're running interactively (stdin is a terminal)
662     // In debug builds with CLAUDECODE, skip interactive prompts (LLM can't type)
663 #ifndef NDEBUG
664     bool skip_interactive = platform_getenv("CLAUDECODE") != NULL;
665 #else
666     bool skip_interactive = false;
667 #endif
668     if (!skip_interactive && platform_is_interactive()) {
669       // Interactive mode - prompt user for confirmation
670       // Lock terminal for the warning message
671       bool previous_terminal_state = log_lock_terminal();
672 
673       log_plain("\n"
674                 "@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@\n"
675                 "@  WARNING: CLIENT AUTHENTICATION REQUIRED                                    @\n"
676                 "@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@\n"
677                 "\n"
678                 "The server requires client authentication (--client-keys enabled),\n"
679                 "but you have not provided a client identity key with --key.\n"
680                 "\n"
681                 "To connect to this server, you need to:\n"
682                 "  1. Generate an Ed25519 key: ssh-keygen -t ed25519\n"
683                 "  2. Add the public key to the server's --client-keys list\n"
684                 "  3. Connect with: ascii-chat client --key /path/to/private/key\n");
685 
686       // Unlock before prompt (prompt_yes_no handles its own terminal locking)
687       log_unlock_terminal(previous_terminal_state);
688 
689       // Prompt user - default is No since this will likely fail
690       if (!platform_prompt_yes_no("Do you want to continue anyway (this will likely fail)", false)) {
691         log_plain("Connection aborted by user.");
692         exit(0); // User declined - exit cleanly
693       }
694 
695       log_plain("Warning: Continuing without client identity key (connection may fail).\n");
696     } else {
697       // Non-interactive mode (background/script) - just log warning and continue
698       log_warn("Non-interactive mode: Continuing without client identity key (connection may fail)");
699     }
700   }
701 
702   // Step 2: Receive auth challenge and send response
703   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO: Sending auth response to server...");
704   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Starting auth response");
705   result = crypto_handshake_client_auth_response_socket(&g_crypto_ctx, socket);
706   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
707     FATAL(result, "Crypto authentication failed");
708   }
709   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO: Auth response sent successfully");
710   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Auth response completed successfully");
711 
712   // Check if handshake completed during auth response (no authentication needed)
713   if (g_crypto_ctx.state == CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
714     // Propagate encryption flag to crypto context
715     g_crypto_ctx.crypto_ctx.encrypt_data = ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(g_crypto_mode);
716     STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(debug, 100 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "client_crypto_handshake",
717                        "Crypto handshake completed successfully (no authentication)");
718     return 0;
719   }
720 
721   // Step 3: Receive handshake complete message
722   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Waiting for handshake complete message");
723   result = crypto_handshake_client_complete_socket(&g_crypto_ctx, socket);
724   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
725     FATAL(result, "Crypto handshake completion failed");
726   }
727 
728   // Propagate encryption flag to crypto context
729   g_crypto_ctx.crypto_ctx.encrypt_data = ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(g_crypto_mode);
730 
731   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(debug, 100 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "client_crypto_handshake", "Crypto handshake completed successfully");
732   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Handshake completed successfully, state=%d", g_crypto_ctx.state);
733   return 0;
734 }




References ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_AUTH,
    ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    AUTH_ALGO_ED25519, AUTH_ALGO_NONE,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::auth_public_key_size,
    buffer_pool_free(), capture_cleanup(),
    CIPHER_ALGO_NONE, CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305,
    protocol_version_packet_t::compression_algorithms,
    protocol_version_packet_t::compression_threshold,
    CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED,
    crypto_handshake_client_auth_response_socket(),
    crypto_handshake_client_complete_socket(),
    crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange_socket(),
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY, crypto_handshake_set_parameters(),
    ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, FATAL,
    protocol_version_packet_t::feature_flags, g_crypto_ctx,
    GET_OPTION, HOST_TO_NET_U16, KEX_ALGO_X25519,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::kex_public_key_size,
    KEY_TYPE_ED25519, lifecycle_is_initialized(),
    log_debug, log_error, log_info,
    log_lock_terminal(), log_plain, log_unlock_terminal(),
    log_warn, MAX_CLIENTS, NET_TO_HOST_U16,
    NS_PER_MS_INT, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS, PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION,
    parse_public_keys(), platform_getenv(),
    platform_is_interactive(), platform_prompt_yes_no(),
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::preferred_auth,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::preferred_cipher,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::preferred_kex,
    protocol_version_packet_t::protocol_revision,
    protocol_version_packet_t::protocol_version, pubkey_to_hex(),
    receive_packet(),
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::requires_verification,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_auth,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_cipher,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_kex,
    send_crypto_capabilities_packet(), send_packet(),
    send_protocol_version_packet(),
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::shared_secret_size,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::signature_size, START_TIMER,
    STOP_TIMER, STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::supported_auth_algorithms,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::supported_cipher_algorithms,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::supported_kex_algorithms,
    protocol_version_packet_t::supports_encryption, and
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::verification_enabled.


Referenced by client_main(),
    connection_attempt_tcp(), and
  server_connection_establish().






int
  client_crypto_init (void )


Initialize client crypto handshake. Initialize client crypto
    handshake




Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure


Definition at line 208 of file
  src/client/crypto.c.


208                              {
209   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Starting crypto initialization");
210 
211   // Reset if already initialized (allows reinit on reconnection)
212   if (lifecycle_reset(&g_crypto_lc)) {
213     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Already initialized, resetting for reinit");
214     crypto_handshake_destroy(&g_crypto_ctx);
215   }
216 
217   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Initializing crypto handshake context");
218   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: crypto_mode=%d (NONE=0, ENCRYPT=1, AUTH=2, FULL=3)", g_crypto_mode);
219 
220   // Check if we have an SSH key, password, or neither
221   int result;
222   bool is_ssh_key = false;
223   private_key_t private_key;
224 
225   // Get string options safely
226   const options_t *opts = options_get();
227   const char *encrypt_key = opts && opts->encrypt_key[0] != '\0' ? opts->encrypt_key : "";
228   const char *password = opts && opts->password[0] != '\0' ? opts->password : "";
229   const char *address = opts && opts->address[0] != '\0' ? opts->address : "localhost";
230   int port = opts ? opts->port : OPT_PORT_INT_DEFAULT;
231   const char *server_key = opts && opts->server_key[0] != '\0' ? opts->server_key : "";
232 
233   // Load client private key if provided via --key
234   if (strlen(encrypt_key) > 0) {
235     // --key supports file-based authentication (SSH keys, GPG keys via gpg:keyid)
236 
237     // For SSH key files (not gpg:keyid format), validate the file exists
238     if (strncmp(encrypt_key, "gpg:", 4) != 0) {
239       if (validate_ssh_key_file(encrypt_key) != 0) {
240         return -1;
241       }
242     }
243 
244     // Parse key (handles SSH files and gpg:keyid format)
245     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Loading private key for authentication: %s", encrypt_key);
246     if (parse_private_key(encrypt_key, &private_key) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
247       log_debug("Successfully parsed SSH private key");
248       log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Parsed key type=%d, KEY_TYPE_ED25519=%d", private_key.type, KEY_TYPE_ED25519);
249       is_ssh_key = true;
250     } else {
251       log_error("Failed to parse SSH key file: %s", encrypt_key);
252       log_error("This may be due to:");
253       log_error("  - Wrong password for encrypted key");
254       log_error("  - Unsupported key type (only Ed25519 is currently supported)");
255       log_error("  - Corrupted key file");
256       log_error("");
257       log_error("Note: RSA and ECDSA keys are not yet supported");
258       log_error("To generate an Ed25519 key: ssh-keygen -t ed25519");
259       return -1;
260     }
261   }
262 
263   if (is_ssh_key) {
264     // Use SSH private key for authentication
265     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Using SSH key for authentication");
266 
267     // Initialize crypto context (generates ephemeral X25519 keys)
268     result = crypto_handshake_init("crypto_client_local", &g_crypto_ctx, false); // false = client
269     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
270       FATAL(result, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake");
271     }
272 
273     // Store the Ed25519 keys for authentication
274     memcpy(&g_crypto_ctx.client_private_key, &private_key, sizeof(private_key_t));
275 
276     // Extract Ed25519 public key from private key
277     g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
278     memcpy(g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.key, private_key.public_key, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
279     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.comment, private_key.key_comment,
280                  sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.comment) - 1);
281 
282     // Extract GPG key ID if this is a GPG key (format: "gpg:KEYID")
283     if (strncmp(encrypt_key, "gpg:", 4) == 0) {
284       const char *key_id = encrypt_key + 4;
285       size_t key_id_len = strlen(key_id);
286       // Accept 8, 16, or 40 character GPG key IDs (short/long/full fingerprint)
287       if (key_id_len == 8 || key_id_len == 16 || key_id_len == 40) {
288         SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id, key_id, sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id));
289         log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Extracted client GPG key ID (%zu chars): %s", key_id_len,
290                   g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id);
291       } else {
292         log_warn("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Invalid GPG key ID length: %zu (expected 8, 16, or 40)", key_id_len);
293         g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id[0] = '\0';
294       }
295     } else {
296       // Not a GPG key, clear the field
297       g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id[0] = '\0';
298     }
299 
300     // SSH key is already configured in the handshake context above
301     // No additional setup needed - SSH keys are used only for authentication
302 
303     // Clear the temporary private_key variable (we've already copied it to g_crypto_ctx)
304     sodium_memzero(&private_key, sizeof(private_key));
305 
306     // If password is also provided, derive password key for dual authentication
307     if (strlen(password) > 0) {
308       log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Password also provided, deriving password key");
309       crypto_result_t crypto_result = crypto_derive_password_key(&g_crypto_ctx.crypto_ctx, password);
310       if (crypto_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
311         log_error("Failed to derive password key: %s", crypto_result_to_string(crypto_result));
312         return -1;
313       }
314       g_crypto_ctx.crypto_ctx.has_password = true;
315       log_debug("Password authentication enabled alongside SSH key");
316     }
317 
318   } else if (strlen(GET_OPTION(password)) > 0) {
319     // Password provided - use password-based initialization
320     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Using password authentication");
321     result = crypto_handshake_init_with_password("crypto_client_local", &g_crypto_ctx, false,
322                                                  GET_OPTION(password)); // false = client
323     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
324       FATAL(result, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake with password");
325     }
326   } else {
327     // No password or SSH key - use standard initialization with random keys
328     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Using standard initialization");
329     result = crypto_handshake_init("crypto_client_local", &g_crypto_ctx, false); // false = client
330     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
331       FATAL(result, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake");
332     }
333   }
334 
335   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: crypto_handshake_init succeeded");
336 
337   // Set up server connection info for known_hosts
338   SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.server_hostname, address, sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.server_hostname) - 1);
339   const char *server_ip = server_connection_get_ip();
340   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: server_connection_get_ip() returned: '%s'", server_ip ? server_ip : "NULL");
341   SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.server_ip, server_ip ? server_ip : "", sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.server_ip) - 1);
342 
343   // Use port from server_connection_set_port() if available (e.g., from WebSocket URL),
344   // otherwise use the port from options (TCP/default path)
345   int connection_port = server_connection_get_port();
346   g_crypto_ctx.server_port = (uint16_t)(connection_port > 0 ? connection_port : port);
347   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Set server_ip='%s', server_port=%u", g_crypto_ctx.server_ip, g_crypto_ctx.server_port);
348 
349   // Configure server key verification if specified
350   if (strlen(server_key) > 0) {
351     g_crypto_ctx.verify_server_key = true;
352     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key, server_key, sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key) - 1);
353     log_debug("Server key verification enabled: %s", server_key);
354   }
355 
356   // If --require-client-verify is set, perform ACDS session lookup for server identity
357   if (GET_OPTION(require_client_verify) && strlen(GET_OPTION(session_string)) > 0) {
358     log_debug("--require-client-verify enabled: performing ACDS session lookup for '%s'", GET_OPTION(session_string));
359 
360     // Connect to ACDS server (configurable via --acds-server and --acds-port options)
361     acds_client_config_t acds_config;
362     acds_client_config_init_defaults(&acds_config);
363     SAFE_STRNCPY(acds_config.server_address, GET_OPTION(discovery_server), sizeof(acds_config.server_address));
364     acds_config.server_port = GET_OPTION(discovery_port);
365     acds_config.timeout_ms = 5 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;
366 
367     // ACDS key verification (optional in debug builds, only if --discovery-service-key is provided)
368     if (strlen(GET_OPTION(discovery_service_key)) > 0) {
369       log_debug("Verifying ACDS server key for %s...", acds_config.server_address);
370       uint8_t acds_pubkey[32];
371       asciichat_error_t verify_result =
372           discovery_keys_verify(acds_config.server_address, GET_OPTION(discovery_service_key), acds_pubkey);
373       if (verify_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
374         log_error("ACDS key verification failed for %s", acds_config.server_address);
375         return -1;
376       }
377       log_debug("ACDS server key verified successfully");
378     }
379 #ifndef NDEBUG
380     else {
381       log_debug("Skipping ACDS key verification (debug build, no --discovery-service-key provided)");
382     }
383 #endif
384 
385     acds_client_t acds_client;
386     asciichat_error_t acds_result = acds_client_connect(&acds_client, &acds_config);
387     if (acds_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
388       log_error("Failed to connect to ACDS server at %s:%d", acds_config.server_address, acds_config.server_port);
389       return -1;
390     }
391 
392     // Perform SESSION_LOOKUP to get server's identity
393     acds_session_lookup_result_t lookup_result;
394     acds_result = acds_session_lookup(&acds_client, GET_OPTION(session_string), &lookup_result);
395     acds_client_disconnect(&acds_client);
396 
397     if (acds_result != ASCIICHAT_OK || !lookup_result.found) {
398       log_error("ACDS session lookup failed for '%s': %s", GET_OPTION(session_string),
399                 lookup_result.found ? "session not found" : "lookup error");
400       return -1;
401     }
402 
403     // Convert server's Ed25519 public key (32 bytes) to hex string
404     char server_key_hex[65]; // 32 bytes * 2 + null terminator
405     for (size_t i = 0; i < 32; i++) {
406       SAFE_SNPRINTF(&server_key_hex[i * 2], 3, "%02x", lookup_result.host_pubkey[i]);
407     }
408     server_key_hex[64] = '\0';
409 
410     // Set expected server key for verification during handshake
411     g_crypto_ctx.verify_server_key = true;
412     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key, server_key_hex, sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key) - 1);
413     log_debug("ACDS session lookup succeeded - server identity will be verified");
414     log_debug("Expected server key (from ACDS): %s", server_key_hex);
415   }
416 
417   // Mark initialization as complete
418   lifecycle_init(&g_crypto_lc, "client_crypto");
419 
420   log_debug("Client crypto handshake initialized");
421   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Initialization complete");
422   return 0;
423 }






References acds_client_config_init_defaults(),
    acds_client_connect(), acds_client_disconnect(),
    acds_session_lookup(), options_state::address,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, crypto_derive_password_key(),
    crypto_handshake_destroy(), crypto_handshake_init(),
    crypto_handshake_init_with_password(), CRYPTO_OK,
    crypto_result_to_string(), discovery_keys_verify(),
    ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, options_state::encrypt_key,
    FATAL, acds_session_lookup_result_t::found,
    g_crypto_ctx, GET_OPTION,
    acds_session_lookup_result_t::host_pubkey,
    private_key_t::key_comment, KEY_TYPE_ED25519,
    lifecycle_init(), lifecycle_reset(), log_debug,
    log_error, log_warn, MS_PER_SEC_INT,
    OPT_PORT_INT_DEFAULT, options_get(),
    parse_private_key(), options_state::password,
    options_state::port, private_key_t::public_key,
    SAFE_SNPRINTF, SAFE_STRNCPY,
    acds_client_config_t::server_address,
    server_connection_get_ip(), server_connection_get_port(),
    options_state::server_key, acds_client_config_t::server_port,
    acds_client_config_t::timeout_ms, private_key_t::type, and
    validate_ssh_key_file().


Referenced by client_main(),
    connection_attempt_tcp(), connection_attempt_websocket(), and
    server_connection_establish().

  client_crypto_set_mode (uint8_t mode)

mode Crypto mode bitmask (ACIP_CRYPTO_*)

  crypto_client_cleanup (void )


Cleanup crypto client resources. Cleanup crypto client
  resources

  src/client/crypto.c.


807                                  {
808   // Shutdown if initialized
809   if (lifecycle_shutdown(&g_crypto_lc)) {
810     crypto_handshake_destroy(&g_crypto_ctx);
811     log_debug("Client crypto handshake cleaned up");
812   }
813 }

  plaintext_len)

Parameters


ciphertext Encrypted data to decrypt

plaintext Output buffer for decrypted data


plaintext_size Size of output buffer


plaintext_len Output length of decrypted data

0 on success, -1 on failure

int
  crypto_client_initiate_rekey (void )


Initiate session rekeying (client-initiated). Initiate session
    rekeying (client-initiated)




Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure


Definition at line 840 of file
  src/client/crypto.c.


840                                        {
841   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_crypto_lc) || !crypto_client_is_ready()) {
842     log_error("Cannot initiate rekey: crypto not initialized or not ready");
843     return -1;
844   }
845 
846   socket_t socket = server_connection_get_socket();
847   if (socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
848     log_error("Cannot initiate rekey: invalid socket");
849     return -1;
850   }
851 
852   asciichat_error_t result = crypto_handshake_rekey_request(&g_crypto_ctx, socket);
853   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
854     log_error("Failed to send REKEY_REQUEST: %d", result);
855     return -1;
856   }
857 
858   return 0;
859 }

    ready




Returns


true if encryption is ready, false otherwise

  crypto_client_process_rekey_request (const uint8_t * packet, size_t
  packet_len)


Process received REKEY_REQUEST packet from server. Process
    received REKEY_REQUEST packet from server




Parameters


packet Packet data


packet_len Packet length


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure


Definition at line 870 of file
  src/client/crypto.c.

871   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_crypto_lc) || !crypto_client_is_ready()) {
872     log_error("Cannot process rekey request: crypto not initialized or not ready");
873     return -1;
874   }
875 
876   asciichat_error_t result = crypto_handshake_process_rekey_request(&g_crypto_ctx, packet, packet_len);
877   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
878     log_error("Failed to process REKEY_REQUEST: %d", result);
879     return -1;
880   }
881 
882   return 0;
883 }

packet Packet data


packet_len Packet length


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure

949

bool crypto_client_should_rekey (void )


Check if session rekeying should be triggered. Check if session
    rekeying should be triggered




Returns


true if rekey should be initiated, false otherwise


Definition at line 826 of file
  src/client/crypto.c.

827   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_crypto_lc) || !crypto_client_is_ready()) {
828     return false;

Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.4
    ascii-chat
